overview: Azorius Flash is a Pioneer deck that builds upon the Azorius Spirits foundation by integrating Enduring Curiosity as a primary card draw engine. It focuses on applying early aggressive pressure with flying creatures while utilizing interaction like Mausoleum Wanderer and Spell Queller to disrupt opponents. The deck aims to control the game, deny opponents space, and efficiently close out matches through consistent board presence.
win_conditions: Attacking from above with flying creatures
Applying consistent pressure with buffed Spirits
Using Mausoleum Wanderer's sacrifice ability to counter key spells while dealing damage

keep_criteria: Hands that let you be aggressive early on
Hands that have answers to enemy removals and interactions
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably duals or fetch/shock sources for W/U), at least one flash enabler (Consult the Star Charts or Aven Interrupter), and a Drannith Magistrate or early removal.
mulligan_criteria: Hands that are quite reactive and only have one turn-3 creature
Hands that can't easily deal with early game threats
Hands that lack an extra land and will likely get stuck
Mulligan pure curve-outs, hands with only flash spells and no lands, or hands with multiple Dranniths but no early interaction or card draw.

examples.reason: Allows you to be aggressive early on and have answers to enemy interactions.
Cannot easily deal with early game threats and relies too much on card draw to progress, giving the opponent time to develop.

early_game: Put creatures in play and attack from above
Protect strategies with counters and Spell Queller
Build the board fast against aggro decks
Turn 1: Play a land, flash in a cheap threat or removal if possible, or hold up mana for interaction.
Turn 2: Flash in a second enabler or threat, deploy Drannith Magistrate to enable graveyard recursion.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast flash spells on the opponent's turn.
Prioritize keeping mana open for flash triggers rather than committing to a single turn's plan.
mid_game: Use Mausoleum Wanderer as a flexible counter spell
Deal with problematic cards using Spell Queller and Skyclave Apparition
Apply pressure with Supreme Phantom and Brazen Borrower
Mana is for flash triggers and interaction, not just casting.
Keep 1-2 cards in hand to react.
Drannith enables recursion, but don't overcommit to the graveyard if hate is present.
Prioritize card advantage engines when the board is stable.
Commit threats when you have removal or flash counterspells to protect them.
late_game: Rely on card draw to progress the game plan
Use The Wandering Emperor to buff creatures and remove threats
Maintain board presence and life total while denying opponent space
A board full of flying threats enabled by Drannith, backed by card advantage from Consult the Star Charts and Aven Interrupter.
Win by trading efficiently and out-grinding with flash value, often closing out in combat after flashing in blockers or removal on the opponent's turn.
When you have card advantage, removal for their threats, and they have no graveyard hate or board wipes.
Go all-in when you can flash in multiple threats on their turn to bypass sweepers or combat damage, forcing them to answer multiple lines simultaneously.

key_cards.name: Enduring Curiosity
key_cards.notes: Draws a card whenever a creature you control deals damage to an opponent.

key_cards.name: Mausoleum Wanderer
key_cards.notes: 1-drop with evasion that gets bigger temporarily; sacrifice ability forces opponent to pay extra value or counters their spell.

key_cards.name: Supreme Phantom
key_cards.notes: Buffs creatures' toughness and power to put pressure on opponent.

key_cards.name: Spell Queller
key_cards.notes: Powerful way to deal with problematic cards and deal considerable damage.

key_cards.name: The Wandering Emperor
key_cards.notes: Buffs creatures, puts more on board, and removes enemy threats.

key_cards.name: Sheltered by Ghosts
key_cards.notes: Removes an enemy permanent while enchanting one of your creatures for more firepower and life.

sequencing_tips: Activate Mausoleum Wanderer's sacrifice ability whenever needed as long as it is on the board
Play it safe and use your creatures well against midrange decks
Stop opponents from resolving Collected Company at all costs
Be careful with your life total against aggressive decks
Common mistake: Flashing in enablers on your turn when you could save mana for the opponent's turn.
Always keep mana open for flash triggers.
protect it or have a backup.
Consult the Star Charts and Aven Interrupter are your card advantage
use them to out-value, not just to cast spells.

matchups.advice: Build your board fast and fit in some Spirits with Supreme Phantom.
Put pressure on them consistently and be careful with their removals.
Attack from above and put pressure on them before they can start playing the game.
Play it safe, use your creatures well, and be careful with problematic creatures.
Be fast, attack them from above, and stop them from resolving a Collected Company at all costs.

Use flash to cast spells on the opponent's turn when they've already spent mana. Use Drannith to cast from the graveyard, bypassing stack-based disruption. Use cheap threats and value engines that don't rely on a single spell, and trade removal efficiently to clear the path for your finishers.

Apply early pressure with flash threats. Use removal on their mana dorks/accelerants. Don't let them stabilize; flash in value to disrupt their plans and force them to answer multiple threats before they can cast their big spells.
Prioritize removal and counterspells. Flash in blockers or removal on their combat step. Use Drannith to recur removal. Stabilize by turn 3-4, then close out with flying threats and card advantage.

Out-value them with card advantage. Flash in threats when they're tapped out. Use Drannith to recur value. Don't overcommit to the stack; trade efficiently and force them to use multiple answers for a single threat.

Win the card advantage war. Prioritize keeping interaction. Use Drannith to recur your enablers. Trade removal efficiently. Flash in threats on their turn to bypass sweepers and force them to answer multiple lines simultaneously.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Scavenging Ooze), board wipes, and counter suites.
Note if opponents are holding up mana for flash responses.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Azorius Flash is a variation of Azorius Spirit - it uses Enduring Curiosity as its main card draw engine, but its foundation is card by card the Spirits foundation.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: You'll put your creatures in play, attack from above, and protect your strategies with counters and Spell Queller.
Supreme Phantom, it is your lord - it buffs your creatures' toughness and power and puts a lot of pressure on your opponent.

Mausoleum Wanderer... has a sacrifice ability that can be considered a counter because it forces your opponent to pay an extra value equal to how Wanderer costs at that moment, or it will counter their spell.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Keep in mind that Mausoleum Wanderer is a counter spell, and that you can activate it whenever you want, as long as it is on the board.
Play it safe, use your creatures well, and be careful with problematic creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse.
stop them from resolving a Collected Company at all costs.
Be careful with your life, as this Rakdos can destroy it quite easily.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Aven Interrupter and Brutal Cathar are very problematic
stop them from resolving a Collected Company at all costs
Be careful with Cavalier of Thorns, their main flying blocker
be careful with problematic creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse
The disruptive Thoughtseize + Duress kit can be quite destructive
be careful with their removals, particularly their global removals
